body:
Team --

We've received FERC's data request on our California market procedural participation, with a December 15 response deadline. I want to get us organized now rather than scramble later.

Key parameters as I understand them:

The request appears to focus on our internal processes for market participation, documentation of decision-making, and potentially our interactions with CAISO and the PX during 2000-2001. This is distinct from the substantive market behavior inquiries we've seen before -- this is about procedures and record-keeping.

My initial read is that we'll need to split the response between regulatory compliance (who handles the technical/procedural factual responses) and government affairs (who contextualizes the strategic and policy-facing elements). But I want to hear from both sides before we lock in that division.

Action items I'd propose we tackle in the next week:

1. Jeff and Paul -- I'd like a preliminary read on which sub-questions you believe implicate legislative/policy judgment calls versus pure factual compliance responses. That distinction will drive our staffing approach.

2. Regulatory compliance team -- I'd like an assessment of what documentation we have readily available versus what will require a more targeted search. Given the time elapsed, I want to flag the risk that some records may be incomplete or harder to reconstruct.

3. Legal -- Need to loop in counsel early on privilege and work product considerations before we start assembling materials.

I'm proposing we set aside time early next week to walk through the request document together and map it against our internal knowledge base. Can we get a call on Monday to start that process?

Jim

Steffes --

Agreed on the need to organize now. A few inputs from the regulatory compliance side:

On the factual vs. strategic distinction: Based on the request scope you've described, I'd estimate roughly 60-70% of the sub-questions will land in factual compliance territory -- process documentation, record-keeping protocols, and procedural adherence during the 2000-2001 period. The remaining 30-40% will implicate policy judgment, particularly any questions touching our engagement with CAISO and PX on market structure matters. We should be prepared to draw a clear line, because FERC staff will probe any gray areas.

On documentation: My immediate concern is retention and reconstruction of internal correspondence from that period. We had active document preservation protocols during 2000-2001, but I want to flag that some of the trading desk communications and market analysis from mid-2000 forward may be incomplete or in archived formats that require IT retrieval. I can initiate a preliminary inventory by document category by end of next week.

On privilege: Strongly endorse getting legal involved before we begin assembling materials. Even factual compliance responses can implicate deliberative process if we're required to explain our reasoning at the time. I'd recommend counsel review the request for privilege flags before we distribute it more broadly to working-level staff.

I can support the Monday call. I'll have preliminary document availability assessment ready by then and can walk through the request with a regulatory lens. If anyone has specific sub-questions they want me to flag in advance of that session, send them over.

Mary

Team --

Good. Mary, thanks for the preliminary split estimate -- the 60-70/30-40 framing is useful and I'll use that as our working baseline when we map the sub-questions on Monday.

A couple of refinements to the action items based on your inputs:

On documentation inventory: Please include archived trading desk communications in that preliminary sweep, but flag them as a separate category. If those require IT retrieval, I want to know the timeline and any access constraints before we commit to including them in the baseline response. We can manage around limitations if we identify them early.

On privilege review: I'll coordinate with legal to have counsel briefed on the request by end of day Friday. Given the 30-40% that touches policy engagement with CAISO and PX, I want counsel's read on where deliberative process concerns are most likely to surface before we circulate anything working-level.

One additional flag I'd add to the Monday agenda: I want us to discuss how we handle any gaps in the record. If documentation is incomplete or unavailable, we need a consistent approach to how we represent that to FERC. I'd rather be transparent about limitations than have staff improvise responses that could be inconsistent across sub-questions.

Monday call: I'll set it for 8:30 AM Pacific to give us a clean hour before the day gets away from us. I'll circulate the dial-in and a clean copy of the request by Friday afternoon so everyone has time to review the actual language over the weekend.

Jeff, Paul -- still need your input on the legislative/policy distinction I raised. If you can get me a preliminary list of which sub-questions you read as implicating policy judgment before Monday, that will let us pre-sort the mapping and move faster in the session.

Jim

Understood on the refinements. I'll treat trading desk communications as a separate retrieval category and will flag IT timeline/access constraints explicitly in the preliminary inventory rather than bundling them with standard retention categories.

On the gap-in-the-record discussion: good call to address it Monday. I'd suggest we frame a consistent approach in advance -- whether that means identifying limitations proactively and characterizing them in the response, or flagging them for legal review before submission. Either way, consistency across sub-questions is critical. FERC staff will notice if the same category of documentation is handled differently across different parts of the response.

I'll have a preliminary document category list ready for Monday with availability status for each. That should give us a concrete starting point for the mapping exercise.

One heads-up: I may have some scheduling conflict Monday morning that I'll confirm by end of day Friday. If I can't make 8:30 Pacific, I'll send a proxy from the regulatory compliance team who can walk through the document inventory assessment in my place.

Mary

Mary --

Understood on the Monday contingency. If you confirm a conflict, please just send whoever's best positioned to walk through the inventory assessment -- I'll take whoever you've got.

Friday deliverables remain:

- Dial-in and clean copy of the request from me
- Counsel briefed on privilege considerations
- Preliminary document category list with availability status from you
- Jeff and Paul's preliminary sub-question sort on policy judgment items

We're in good shape to advance on Monday. I'll be in touch if anything changes on my end before end of day.

Jim

Confirmed. I'll have the preliminary document category list with availability status to you by Friday as outlined. I'll also confirm my Monday availability by end of day Friday.

Mary
